citation: “Were You Paying Attention?!” by Beth and Angel Make Games
summary:
# Summary

The provided text is a descriptive overview of the tabletop game *Were You Paying Attention?!* created by Beth and Angel Make Games. It does not present empirical research, data analysis, or experimental findings. Instead, it functions as a promotional artifact and resource guide associated with the GameRT (Game Reading Tour) initiative, likely linked to library services given the contact email `gamert@ala.org` (American Library Association).

The core objective of the text is to introduce the game’s premise: a competitive experience where players attempt to overwhelm opponents with information to test their attention spans. The authors frame this mechanic as a lesson on the "literal cost" of focus, warning against overtaxing listeners. The text explicitly states that the only way to win is for others to lose, characterizing the environment as "competitive."

Regarding methodology and data, there are none. The text does not report on player performance, cognitive load measurements, or user feedback. Consequently, no sample sizes, participant demographics, or statistical results are available. The "Population" section is therefore not applicable in a research sense; however, the text identifies the beneficiaries of the game’s sales as trans individuals in Florida, supported by donations to Zebra Youth and Transinclusive Group.

The findings section is limited to descriptive claims about the game’s mechanics and its place within the broader tabletop gaming landscape. The authors provide lists of "Play Alikes" (e.g., *Fiasco*, *Codenames*, *Hanabi*) to help potential players understand the game’s genre, which includes communication limits, memory, and storytelling mechanisms. Additionally, "Read Alikes" are suggested to contextualize the narrative aspects, citing works on storytelling by Meg Bowles, Margot Leitman, and others. The text also references external resources on cognitive load theory in roleplaying games, suggesting a theoretical underpinning for the game’s design, though no data is presented to validate this connection.

A significant portion of the text is dedicated to community standards and safety protocols, which are highly relevant to library-based gaming services. It lists specific "Safety Tools" such as Script Change and The Door Is Open, and references the TTRPG Safety Toolkit. Furthermore, it outlines the "Game RT Twitch Policy," which mandates kindness, prohibits hate speech, and provides procedures for handling harassment via moderators or direct messages. This indicates a strong emphasis on creating a safe, inclusive environment for participants, particularly given the charitable focus on trans rights.

The implications drawn from the text are primarily practical and ethical. First, it positions *Were You Paying Attention?!* as an educational tool for understanding attention limits through play. Second, it demonstrates how library services (via GameRT) can leverage gaming for social good, specifically by bundling game sales with charitable donations to support marginalized communities. Third, the inclusion of safety tools and strict chat policies implies that facilitators of games-based library services must proactively manage community interactions to ensure inclusivity and prevent harassment.

In summary, this text is not a study but a resource document. It provides essential context for understanding how a specific game was deployed within a charitable and community-focused framework. For a literature review on Games-Based Library Services (GBLS), its value lies in illustrating the integration of safety protocols, charitable fundraising, and cognitive-themed gameplay in library-led initiatives, rather than offering empirical evidence of learning outcomes or user engagement metrics.
sourceText:
“Were You Paying Attention?!” by Beth and Angel Make Games
Were You Paying Attention?! is a game about, well, paying attention! It's a reminder that paying attention has a literal cost —focus— and that it's important not to overtax listeners
with too much information. How do you learn that, you ask? By overwhelming your opponents with information, of course! This is a competitive world, and the only way to win is for everyone else to lose,
obviously. Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A The Game “Were You Paying Attention?!” by Beth and Angel Make Games. Bundle Information: For the last few hours of Friday 4/7/2023 Were you Paying Attention?! is a part of the TTRPGs for Trans Rights in Florida! over on itch.io. You can purchase it along with 505 other games for $5 and support both Zebra Youth and Transinclusive Group,“two charities that work directly with trans folx on the ground in Florida, to get the most direct aid to those who need it most.”

Play Alikes ● TTRPGs ○ Fiasco, Bully Pulpit Games ○ Epitaph, Less Than Three GAmes ○ Stealing Stories, Monte Cook Games ● Board Games ○ Cameleon, Big Potato Games ○ Codenames, Czech Games ○ Decrypto, Le Scorpion Masqué ○ Fake Artist Goes to New York, Oink Games ○ Hanabi, RNR Games ○ The Moth Presents: A Game of Storytelling ○ Sounds Fishy, Big Potato Games ○ Telestrations, The Op (USAOPOLY) ○ Wavelength: Tabletop & Mobile ○ Word Slam, Brädspel.se, KOSMOS ○ Mechanism lists on BoardGameGeek: Communication Limits, Memory, Storytelling
Read Alikes ● How to Tell a Story by Meg Bowles, Catherine Burns, & et. al. ● Long Story Short by Margot Leitman ● Out on the Wire by Jessica Abel ● The Science of Storytelling by Will Storr
Website Articles ● Alphabet Improv Game ● The Bardic Inquiry - Cognitive Load Theory in Roleplaying Games ● The Moth | Storytelling Tips & Tricks ● Seven Principles of Storytelling - Roleplaying Tips
Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive

Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
